name: reverse-geocoder
documentation:
Reverse Geocoder
-----------------
Reverse Geocoder takes a latitude / longitude coordinate and returns the nearest town/city.
This library improves on an existing library called reverse_geocode developed by Richard Penman in the following ways:
1. Besides city and country, this library also returns the administrative 1 & 2 regions, latitude and longitude
2. The performance is much faster since a parallelized K-D tree is implemented 
(See https://github.com/thampiman/reverse-geocoder for performance comparison)

Supports Python 2 and 3. You can also load a custom data source. Fore more help, see https://github.com/thampiman/reverse-geocoder.

Example usage:
    >>> import reverse_geocoder as rg
    >>> coordinates = (51.5214588,-0.1729636),(9.936033, 76.259952),(37.38605,-122.08385)
    >>> rg.search(coordinates)
    [{'name': 'Bayswater', 
      'cc': 'GB', 
      'lat': '51.51116',
      'lon': '-0.18426', 
      'admin1': 'England', 
      'admin2': 'Greater London'}, 
     {'name': 'Cochin', 
      'cc': 'IN', 
      'lat': '9.93988',
      'lon': '76.26022', 
      'admin1': 'Kerala', 
      'admin2': 'Ernakulam'},
     {'name': 'Mountain View', 
      'cc': 'US', 
      'lat': '37.38605',
      'lon': '-122.08385', 
      'admin1': 'California', 
      'admin2': 'Santa Clara County'}]

modules.name: reverse_geocoder.cKDTree_MP
documentation:
A Parallelised Implementation of K-D Trees

Code extended from http://folk.uio.no/sturlamo/python/multiprocessing-tutorial.pdf

functions.name: reverse_geocoder.cKDTree_MP.shmem_as_nparray
documentation: Function that converts a shared memory array (multiprocessing.Array) to a numpy array

functions.name: reverse_geocoder.cKDTree_MP._pquery
documentation: Function that parallelly queries the K-D tree based on chunks of data returned by the scheduler

functions.name: reverse_geocoder.cKDTree_MP.num_cpus
documentation:
Function to get the number of CPUs / cores. This is used to determine the number of processes to spawn.
Default (if not implemented) = 2

classes.name: reverse_geocoder.cKDTree_MP.cKDTree_MP

methods.name: reverse_geocoder.cKDTree_MP.cKDTree_MP.__init__
documentation:
Class Instantiation
Arguments are based on scipy.spatial.cKDTree class

methods.name: reverse_geocoder.cKDTree_MP.cKDTree_MP.pquery
documentation: Function to parallelly query the K-D Tree
The parallelised cKDTree class

classes.name: reverse_geocoder.cKDTree_MP.Scheduler
documentation:
Scheduler that returns chunks of data to be queries on the K-D Tree.
The number of chunks is determined by the number of processes.

modules.name: reverse_geocoder
documentation:
A Fast, Offline Reverse Geocoder in Python

A Python library for offline reverse geocoding. It improves on an existing library
called reverse_geocode developed by Richard Penman.

functions.name: reverse_geocoder.singleton
documentation: Function to get single instance of the RGeocoder class

functions.name: reverse_geocoder.rel_path
documentation: Function that gets relative path to the filename

functions.name: reverse_geocoder.get
documentation: Function to query for a single coordinate

functions.name: reverse_geocoder.search
documentation: Function to query for a list of coordinates

classes.name: reverse_geocoder.RGeocoder

methods.name: reverse_geocoder.RGeocoder.__init__
documentation:
Class Instantiation
Args:
mode (int): Library supports the following two modes:
            - 1 = Single-threaded K-D Tree
            - 2 = Multi-threaded K-D Tree (Default)
verbose (bool): For verbose output, set to True
stream (io.StringIO): An in-memory stream of a custom data source

methods.name: reverse_geocoder.RGeocoder.query
documentation:
Function to query the K-D tree to find the nearest city
Args:
coordinates (list): List of tuple coordinates, i.e. [(latitude, longitude)]

methods.name: reverse_geocoder.RGeocoder.load
documentation:
Function that loads a custom data source
Args:
stream (io.StringIO): An in-memory stream of a custom data source.
                      The format of the stream must be a comma-separated file
                      with header containing the columns defined in RG_COLUMNS.

methods.name: reverse_geocoder.RGeocoder.extract
documentation:
Function loads the already extracted GeoNames cities file or downloads and extracts it if
it doesn't exist locally
Args:
local_filename (str): Path to local RG_FILE
The main reverse geocoder class
